questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using a singly linked list over an array?
options: Ability to insert or delete elements at any position

questions.question: In a singly linked list, what happens when the last node points to `NULL`?
options: The list has reached its maximum capacity

questions.question: How can you implement a stack using a linked list?
options: Using the head node as the top of the stack
Using the tail node as the top of the stack
Using a separate data structure to keep track of the top node
Using an array and index as the top of the stack
questions.explanation: Using the tail node as the top of the stack

questions.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a linked list from the head to the tail?
How can you implement a queue using a linked list?
options: Using a separate data structure to keep track of the front and rear nodes
Using the head node as the front of the queue and the tail node as the rear
Using an array and index as the front and rear of the queue
questions.explanation: Using the head node as the front of the queue and the tail node as the rear
